original_hypothesis.title: BlastOnboardingBoot setReady Can Be Toggled After Users Claim
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: setReady() allows the owner to toggle ready between true and false at any time (state must be Closed). After some users claim with ready=true, the owner can set ready=false, change the staking contract via setStaking() (which only blocks when ready=true), then set ready=true again. Early claimers staked to the old contract, later claimers to the new one. Early claimers may lose access to rewards or be stranded in the wrong staking contract.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: setReady has no irreversibility guard. setStaking is blocked by `if (ready) revert`, but ready can be toggled. Combined, this allows mid-process staking contract changes.

proof_of_signal.pattern: setReady(bool _ready) allows toggling without irreversibility check

preconditions: Owner calls setReady(true), users begin claiming
Owner calls setReady(false), then setStaking(newStaking), then setReady(true)

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: ready can be set to false after users have already claimed
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: staking contract can be swapped mid-claim process
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No check that claimed[user] count is 0 before allowing ready=false

analysis_questions: Is there any off-chain governance constraint preventing this toggle?
Would early claimers lose rewards in the old staking contract?
Does the new staking contract receive the remaining pool shares?
validation_requirements: Simulate: setReady(true), user1.claim(), setReady(false), setStaking(new), setReady(true), user2.claim()
Verify user1 and user2 are in different staking contracts

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Malicious owner sets ready=true, waits for some users to claim, sets ready=false, deploys a new staking contract that extracts value, calls setStaking(malicious), sets ready=true. Remaining users' claims go to the malicious staking contract.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Later claimers' LP shares directed to malicious staking contract
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All users who haven't yet claimed

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for ready toggle after partial claims

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Observable by monitoring staking contract address changes

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
